review_summary.one_line: 2 material categories mapped to forecast-relevant competitors; Services needs user review due to mixed evidence.
highlights: iPhone pairing uses direct premium smartphone overlap.
Services pairing is marked medium confidence because business model overlap is partial.
warnings: Services has mixed source quality and should be reviewed before Step 4.

sources.name: Apple FY2025 Form 10-K
sources.excerpt: Products and Services net sales are disclosed separately.

sources.name: Samsung Electronics Annual Report
sources.excerpt: Mobile experience includes smartphones and connected devices.

sources.name: Digital services market tracker
sources.excerpt: Major platforms compete across app stores, payments, streaming, and subscriptions.

categories.basis_for_pairing: Samsung is the closest direct premium smartphone competitor with global scale.
categories.verification_note: Official filings support category presence; market position still depends on external share data.

rivalry.justification: Premium smartphones compete on ecosystem, hardware refresh cycles, and price bands.

new_entrants.justification: Brand, supply chain, OS ecosystem, and distribution scale create high entry barriers.

suppliers.justification: Specialized components matter, but Apple has scale and supplier diversification.

buyers.justification: Consumers have alternatives, but switching costs and ecosystem lock-in reduce churn.

substitutes.justification: Tablets and PCs complement smartphones rather than replacing primary mobile usage.

categories.basis_for_pairing: Alphabet overlaps across app distribution, payments, subscriptions, advertising, and cloud-adjacent services.
categories.verification_note: Overlap is broad but not one-to-one; user review should confirm the carry-forward competitor.

rivalry.justification: Platform services compete for developer economics, subscriptions, and advertising budgets.

new_entrants.justification: New services can launch quickly, but platform distribution and trust are hard to replicate.

suppliers.justification: Content owners and developers can influence economics in subscriptions and app marketplaces.

buyers.justification: Users can switch individual services while remaining inside the device ecosystem.

substitutes.justification: Third-party apps, web services, and competing bundles can substitute for individual services.
